title: Double-Fry-Indulgent Japanese Beef Flank Steak Udon
description: Indulge in this pungent and creamy Japanese dish featuring tender beef flank steak and crispy udon noodles, perfect for a high-protein dinner
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted beef broth to a boil and cook the udon noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Season the beef flank steak with salt, white pepper, and a pinch of sugar.
Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the beef flank steak for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned.
Transfer the beef flank steak to a plate and set aside to rest.
Heat another 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in the same skillet and add the sliced green onions. Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the toasted sesame seeds, grated daikon radish, and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the double-fry sauce to the skillet and stir to combine with the green onions and sesame seeds.
Return the beef flank steak to the skillet and coat with the double-fry sauce.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the beef flank steak is heated through and the double-fry sauce is bubbly.
Add the cooked udon noodles to the skillet and toss with the beef flank steak and double-fry sauce.
Serve immediately and garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
